vision_report.compositional_truth_check: The registry is largely non-representative of the current visual state. P3 (Pothos) and its anchor (White Rabbit) are absent from the current frame. P1 (String of Nickels) is not visible. P2/P4 (Mexican Mint/Silver Guest) are not identifiable as healthy specimens.

inventory_reconciliation.P3: Systemic Loss (Rabbit anchor and Pothos missing)

biome_observations.soil_texture: Consistently dark, damp, and undisturbed across all frames.
biome_observations.debris: Presence of crushed eggshells (calcium amendment) noted in the black pot.

temporal_deltas.t-5_to_t-4: Removal of White Rabbit anchor and Pothos specimen.
temporal_deltas.t-4_to_current: Progressive disappearance of remaining leaf structures; total biome collapse.

vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome has suffered a total systemic failure. All biological specimens have been removed or have perished. The environment is currently inert.
vision_report.anomalies: The introduction of crushed eggshells suggests an attempt at soil remediation or nutrient supplementation that failed to prevent the loss of the specimens.
vision_report.narrative_description: The audit reveals a catastrophic loss of the botanical collection. Over the 5-day observation period, the specimens (Pothos, String of Nickels, and others) were systematically removed or withered. The current state is a collection of empty pots containing only soil and calcium-rich debris (eggshells). The absence of the scale anchor (White Rabbit) confirms a significant intervention or clearing of the desk space.
